reason: While the command 'grub2-setpassword' is standard, modifying the bootloader configuration carries an inherent risk of preventing the system from booting correctly or locking out administrators from accessing the GRUB menu if the password is lost or the configuration is corrupted. Per policy, changes to the bootloader are high-risk.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential for boot failure if configuration is corrupted; Risk of losing physical/console access to the boot menu; Requires manual verification of boot success
